items.headline: Trade body closes first African ministerial without agreement on digital commerce or agriculture
items.body: The World Trade Organization's 14th conference ended without consensus after US-Brazil deadlock over e-commerce duties and investment rules. The impasse leaves key moratoria set to expire and defers decisions to Geneva talks.

items.headline: Philippines declares first national energy emergency as Asia braces for extended fuel crisis
items.body: Manila became the first country to invoke emergency powers as reserves drop to 45 days and fuel stations close nationwide. Southeast Asia faces coordinated shortages as transport-dependent economies scramble for alternative suppliers amid strait disruptions.

items.headline: China factory activity returns to expansion with strongest gain in 12 months
items.body: Manufacturing PMI jumped to 50.4 in March from February's contraction as new orders and export demand rebounded post-Lunar New Year. The largest monthly gain in a year offers reassurance to commodity exporters and regional supply chains entering the second quarter.

items.headline: Thailand approves new cabinet as government prepares economic response to regional energy shock
items.body: Royal endorsement of Prime Minister Anutin's 35-member cabinet clears way for policy statement next week focused on stimulus and fuel price management. The formation concludes three-week transition amid mounting pressure on transport costs and household budgets.

meanwhile.text: Eurovision announces first Asian edition for Bangkok in November with ten countries confirmed
NASA's Artemis II prepares for Wednesday launch sending four astronauts around the Moon for first time since 1972
Global helium shortage threatens MRI access and semiconductor production as Qatar accounts for third of world supply
Macao hosts Maritime Silk Road cultural parade with 1,600 performers from Asia, Europe and Africa
